DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF SANDEEP HUNDAL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH THE DEFENDANT CERTAIN DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORIES WHICH SEEK "ANY AND ALL" DOCUMENTS IN A SONG-BEVERLY CONSUMER WARRANTY ACT ACTION IS OVERBROAD AND POTENTIALLY COVERS MILLIONS OF DOCUMENTS. THE COURT INTENDS TO MODIFY, WHEN NEEDED, EACH SPECIFIC DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORY TO FOCUS ON ALLEGATIONS OF THE SUBJECT VEHICLE'S ALLEGED TRANSMISSION PROBLEM. THE COURT FINDS DONLEN V. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, 217 CAL. APP. 4TH 138 (2013) AND DOPPES V. BENTLEY MOTORS, INC., 174 CAL. APP 4TH 967 (2009) SUPPORT THE BROAD SCOPE OF DISCOVERABLE DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE REQUESTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH DEFENDANT A PROTECTIVE ORDER/CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER IS APPROPRIATE FOR MANY OF THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED. PROPOSED ORDER SHALL SUBMITTED ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 1, 2015. DEFENDANT SHALL BE PERMITTED TO PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION ON PROTECTIVE ORDER ISSUES. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, REPORTER: KATHRYN LLOYD, CSR# 5955, TEL NO. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Continued To Aug-20-15 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NEIL GIELEGHEM, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Neil Gieleghem, Tel No. 310-284-3252 and atty. BENJEMAN BECK, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices APC, Tel No. 310-277-1040 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PAUL EFSTRATIS, ESQ. and RYAN J. GREENSPAN, ESQ. of LeClairRyan LLP, Tel No. 415-913-4957 appearing for the Defendant (Mr. Greenspan appearing telephonically).

RULING - Matter argued by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF SANDEEP HUNDAL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, the motion to compel is granted. The Court agrees with the defendant certain document request categories which seek "any and all" documents in a Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act action is overbroad and potentially covers millions of documents. The Court intends to modify, when needed, each specific document request category to focus on allegations of the subject vehicle's alleged transmission problem. The Court finds Donlen v. Ford Motor Company, 217 Cal. App. 4th 138 (2013) and Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App 4th 967 (2009) support the broad scope of discoverable documentary evidence requested. The Court agrees with defendant a Protective Order/Confidentiality Order is appropriate for many of the documents to be produced. Proposed order shall submitted on or before September 1, 2015. Defendant shall be permitted to provide a supplemental declaration on protective order issues. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR# 5955, Tel No. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One

APPEARANCES: Atty. BENJEMAN R. BECK, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices, APC, Tel No. 310-277-1040 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. RYAN GREENSPAN, ESQ. of LeClair Ryan LLP, Tel. 415-913-4965 appearing for Defendant Ford Motor Company.

Parties stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. PLAINTIFF SANDEEP HUNDAL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is continued to August 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302, Discovery Calendar. Plaintiff 's counsel is to ensure that the court is provided with a courtesy copy of the notice of motion, separate statement declaration of Jacob Cutler, and request for judicial notice that were not available for review by the pro tem judge. Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kinsley, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Holly Sayers, CSR #13678, Tel No. 877-451-1580. (302/JPT)
